mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and declining consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, which creates positive short-term momentum for equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during supply-constrained inflationary periods or shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the consumer price index above historical thresholds triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >60% of periods when inflation exceeds the threshold, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion that increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of GDP expansion quarters or the correlation between GDP growth and equity returns falls below 0.10 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side disruptions and weakening economic activity, which triggers risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The market exhibits positive 5-day returns in >60% of severe industrial production contractions, or the signal shows no predictive edge in walk-forward validation.
